config.seed_text: An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer.

model.notes: Fastest baseline; useful as a collapse-prone small reference model.

records.reflection_text: Text-only code is easy to read at this point because only 1 of 9 texts with multiple lines make one move (e.. 3) or fewer characters long enough for each statement alone to be readable as well on paper without being completely incoherent . For example some sentences might appear confusing depending upon whether you know where your sentence ends if they aren't just repeated before either paragraph goes into writing form — I don’t care what type of word there exists right now! The other way around, we can change our definitions so people will find out when anything happens anyway using their current vocabulary instead :)
records.output_text: The new syntax also adds an entirely different look that makes them easier than previous languages :

reflection_generation.prompt:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Analyze the following text and do two things:
1. State its strongest recurring ideas in 2-4 short bullet points.
2. Produce a revised continuation that makes those ideas more structurally coherent and more surprising.

TEXT:
An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer.

2. Produce a revised continuation that makes those ideas more structurally coherent and more surprising.

TEXT:
An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer.
Text-only code is easy to read at this point because only 1 of 9 texts with multiple lines make one move (e.. 3) or fewer characters long enough for each statement alone to be readable as well on paper without being completely incoherent . For example some sentences might appear confusing depending upon whether you know where your sentence ends if they aren't just repeated before either paragraph goes into writing form — I don’t care what type of word there exists right now! The other way around, we can change our definitions so people will find out when anything happens anyway using their current vocabulary instead :)

The other way around, we can change our definitions so people will find out when anything happens anyway using their current vocabulary instead :)
